# `liber:audio@1`

Generated reference for the `liber:audio` package (`audio.lsidl`). See [the LSIDL language](../../../LSIDL.md).

LiberSystem audio package - audio playback service.

## Imports

- `error` from [`liber:base@1`](/docs/gen/liber/base/v1.md)

## Interfaces

### interface `audio`

AudioService: headless PCM playback over the virtio-sound device. `beep` queues a tone of the given frequency (Hz) and duration (milliseconds) into the same mixer - the device is reached as a capability (the channel this interface is served on), never as ambient device access. `open-stream` returns a sub-channel served as `pcm-stream`; rates from 8 through 48 kHz and one or two channels are accepted, then converted to the hardware's fixed 48 kHz stereo periods.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `beep` | `beep(freq: u16, millis: u32) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 2 | `open-stream` | `open-stream(rate: u32, channels: u8) -> result<handle<channel>, error>` |  |

### interface `pcm-stream`

A playback stream returned by `audio.open-stream`. Samples are signed 16-bit little-endian, interleaved by channel; one `write` contains whole sample frames. `write` returns the number of source frames accepted (possibly a prefix) only after bounded playback capacity is available, making synchronous IPC backpressure the playback clock. Closing the channel is equivalent to `close`; explicit close drains accepted samples first.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `write` | `write(data: buffer) -> result<u32, error>` |  |
| 2 | `close` | `close() -> result<unit, error>` |  |
